content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (Apr 19 - 25, 2026)

## ­¤ÜĆ Highlights
The development cycle for April 19ŌĆō25, 2026, was defined by a rigorous focus on infrastructure stability and the expansion of the LifeOps ecosystem. The team successfully integrated new agent-native prediction market capabilities while simultaneously performing a massive overhaul of core dependencies across the Rust, Python, and frontend stacks. These efforts, combined with refinements to agent orchestration and synthesis logic, have significantly bolstered the framework's reliability and cross-platform compatibility.

## ­¤øĀ’ĖÅ Key Developments

### Agent Orchestration & LifeOps
The team prioritized enhancing agent autonomy and scheduling capabilities.
*   **LifeOps Expansion:** The `app-lifeops` ecosystem received significant upgrades, including new actions for calendar management, travel booking, and cross-platform gateway support ([#6984](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6984)).
*   **Orchestration Logic:** Synthesis logic was refined by implementing subagent `end_turn` reading and integrating `installTaskHeartbeat` into swarm synthesis ([#6964](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6964), [#6972](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6972)).
*   **Discord/Telegram Integration:** Discord routing for LifeOps was stabilized, and Milady CI regressionsŌĆöspecifically regarding Telegram export and website blockersŌĆöwere resolved ([#6971](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6971), [#7008](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7008)).

### New Features & Core Logic
*   **Prediction Markets:** Introduced `plugin-hiveexchange` to enable agent-native prediction market participation ([#6963](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6963)).
*   **LLM Compatibility:** Added a `PROMPT_OUTPUT_FORMAT` environment variable to improve support for models like Gemini 2.5 Pro and Llama that encounter issues with default TOON encapsulation ([#6978](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6978)).
*   **Database Reliability:** Fixed `InMemoryDatabaseAdapter` to correctly handle `roomId` changes and implemented descending memory sorting to ensure consistency with `plugin-sql` ([#6965](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6965), [#7000](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7000)).

### Dependency & Infrastructure Maintenance
The project underwent a comprehensive dependency audit to ensure security and compatibility.
*   **Core Updates:** Extensive updates were applied to Rust crates (e.g., `tokio`, `reqwest`, `uuid`), Python benchmarks (`torch`, `transformers`), and frontend SDKs (Uniswap, Capacitor, and various `androidx` libraries).
*   **Tooling:** Updates to `bun`, `vitest`, `typescript`, and `tsup` were processed to maintain a modern development environment ([#6973](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6973), [#6982](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6982), [#6996](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6996)).

## ­¤Éø Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
*   **Agent Commerce:** Multiple redundant proposals regarding Merxex integration were closed following maintainer feedback ([#6961](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6961), [#6966](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6966), [#6967](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6967), [#6977](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6977)).
*   **Data Privacy:** A request for a PII sanitization layer was closed, though it remains a topic of active interest ([#7007](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7007)).

### New & Active Issues
*   **PII Sanitization:** A new issue was opened to implement a PII sanitization layer to protect user data before it reaches LLM providers ([#7007](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7007)).
*   **Telegram Optimization:** A follow-up issue was created regarding Telegram read receipts, specifically to fetch by message IDs to avoid inefficient nested lookups ([#7009](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7009)).

## ­¤Æ¼ Community & Collaboration
The week was characterized by high-volume maintenance and infrastructure-focused collaboration. The rapid closure of redundant Merxex integration proposals indicates a clear maintainer preference for standardized, vetted approaches to agent commerce. The high volume of dependency-related PRs suggests a coordinated effort to keep the framework's diverse tech stack (Rust, Python, TypeScript) aligned and secure. The community remains highly active in identifying edge cases for LLM output formatting and data privacy, as evidenced by the ongoing discussions surrounding PII sanitization.
